summary: PR 2207 matched the Mario Cape item object by adding an ItemStateTable in itmariocape.c, moving the existing itMarioCape_Logic41_Destroyed function earlier in the translation unit, changing one sdata2 float symbol at 0x804DCFB8 from global it_804DCFB8 to local @151, and flipping configure.py for melee/it/items/itmariocape.c from NonMatching to Matching. The bot report recorded linked code +816 bytes, matched data +32 bytes, and one new match for main/melee/it/items/itmariocape .data from 0.00% to 100.00%.

key_files.role: Primary source file; added ItemStateTable it_803F70F8 and relocated itMarioCape_Logic41_Destroyed within the file without changing its body.
Symbol metadata update; changed .sdata2:0x804DCFB8 from global it_804DCFB8 to local @151.
Build matching status update; changed melee/it/items/itmariocape.c from Object(NonMatching) to Object(Matching).

what_changed: Added ItemStateTable it_803F70F8[] with two entries using itMariocape_UnkMotion1_Anim and NULL callbacks in src/melee/it/items/itmariocape.c.
Moved itMarioCape_Logic41_Destroyed(Item_GObj* gobj) from the end of itmariocape.c to immediately after it_802B2560; the function body shown in the diff remained the same.
Changed config/GALE01/symbols.txt entry at .sdata2:0x804DCFB8 from global symbol it_804DCFB8 to local label @151, retaining type object, size 0x4, align 4, data float.
Changed configure.py so melee/it/items/itmariocape.c is listed as Object(Matching) instead of Object(NonMatching).
decomp-dev bot reported linked code +816 bytes, matched data +32 bytes, linked data +40 bytes, and one new match for main/melee/it/items/itmariocape .data from 0.00% to 100.00%.
smart_moves: Kept the existing itMarioCape_Logic41_Destroyed logic intact while changing its placement, suggesting the match issue was ordering/layout-related rather than behavior-related.
Used symbol metadata to mark a float at .sdata2:0x804DCFB8 as a local label instead of compensating in C with an extra named global.
Updated configure.py only after the item object was presented as matching, keeping the matching status tied to the source and symbol changes.

decomp_lessons.lesson: For item object matches, data/table placement and symbol metadata can be part of the match surface, not just function bodies.
If a function body already has the right semantics, try source-order/layout adjustments before rewriting logic; this PR moved itMarioCape_Logic41_Destroyed without changing its shown body.
When matching an item file introduces a local float reference, correcting symbols.txt scope/name to a local label may be preferable to keeping an address-style global symbol.

naming_conventions.name: it_803F70F8
naming_conventions.observation: The newly added ItemStateTable uses an address-style name rather than an invented semantic table name.
The .sdata2 float at 0x804DCFB8 was converted from global it_804DCFB8 to local label @151.

naming_conventions.name: ip
naming_conventions.observation: The preserved destroy callback uses Item* ip = GET_ITEM(gobj), matching common short local role naming for an item pointer.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Add the item state table in source rather than leaving the table only as unmatched data.
Adjust translation-unit source order by relocating an existing callback function while preserving its body.
Correct data symbol scope/name metadata for a .sdata2 float from global address-style symbol to local label.
Flip the object from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py after the source and symbol changes.

notes: PR body is empty and there are no review comments, so author rationale and reviewer intent are not available.
The bot report provides progress metrics, but the slice does not include objdiff output or a full build log.

The diff supports the changed files and code/symbol edits directly; causality between each individual edit and the final match is inferred only at a PR-level from the merged matching-status change and bot report.

accepted_candidate_records.title: itmariocape match combined source data table recovery, local symbol metadata, and matching-status update
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2207 added ItemStateTable it_803F70F8 to src/melee/it/items/itmariocape.c, moved the existing itMarioCape_Logic41_Destroyed callback earlier in the file, changed .sdata2:0x804DCFB8 from global it_804DCFB8 to local @151 in symbols.txt, and marked itmariocape.c as Object(Matching). The bot reported one new match for main/melee/it/items/itmariocape .data from 0.00% to 100.00%.

source_update_candidates.title: itmariocape.c marked matching in PR 2207
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2207 changed configure.py so melee/it/items/itmariocape.c is Object(Matching) instead of Object(NonMatching).
source_update_candidates.reason: This is a concrete path-status fact from the diff, but should be curated against current source before promotion.

source_update_candidates.title: .sdata2:0x804DCFB8 local label in itmariocape context
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2207 changed config/GALE01/symbols.txt for .sdata2:0x804DCFB8 from global it_804DCFB8 to local @151, preserving object size 0x4, align 4, data float.
source_update_candidates.reason: Symbol metadata is source-owned and should be checked against current symbols before any persistent path fact is added.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that adding ItemStateTable alone caused the match; the PR also changed symbol metadata, source ordering, and configure status.
Do not treat the absence of review comments as approval of a general tactic beyond the merged PR evidence.
Do not promote a broad rule that functions should be reordered for matching; this slice only supports that it happened for itMarioCape_Logic41_Destroyed in itmariocape.c.
